exercises.title: A: Word formation
exercises.instructions: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
exercises.studentInstruction: Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap in the same line.
exercises.passage:
Keeping in touch
Good (1) ...... (COMMUNICATE) is important when people live far apart. A clear (2) ...... (TRANSLATE) can help people understand messages in another language. Sometimes a word or (3) ...... (EXPRESS) sounds natural in one language but strange in another. A good (4) ...... (SPEAK) knows how to make the message sound clear. Online messages can be (5) ...... (PREDICT), so we don't always know when someone will reply. A strong Internet (6) ...... (CONNECT) makes video calls easier. I can say with (7) ...... (CERTAIN) that technology has changed friendships. People can now share (8) ...... (INFORM) instantly, write (9) ...... (SECRET) to each other, and stay in contact with friends all over the world. It has (10) ...... (CERTAIN) made communication faster.
explanations: Noun from EXPRESS → a word or **expression**.
Adjective from PREDICT → messages can be **unpredictable**.
Adverb from SECRET → write **secretly** to each other.
Adverb from CERTAIN → has **certainly** made communication faster.

exercises.exerciseTypeName: B: Write one word in each gap
exercises.title: B: Write one word in each gap
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: When does the next issue of that science magazine come ...... ?
The storm damaged the line and cut the Internet ...... .
I'm busy now, but I'll call you ...... after lunch.
He got angry and hung ...... before I could explain.
Can you print this booking confirmation ...... for me?
She logged ...... the school website to check her homework.
Please fill ...... your name and address clearly.
items.explanation: Complete a form → fill **in** your name.

items.sentence: After checking my messages, I logged ...... and shut down the computer.

exercises.title: C: Key Word Transformation
exercises.instructions: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.
exercises.studentInstruction: Complete each second sentence using the word given, so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence. Write between two and five words.

items.sentence:
Laura sent me an invitation by e-mail. **received**
I ...... Laura.
items.explanation: Passive receive → **received an invitation from** Laura.

items.sentence:
It isn't necessary to bring your own headphones. **have**
You ...... bring your own headphones.
items.explanation: Not necessary → **don't have to** bring headphones.

items.sentence:
Nina is able to speak and understand German. **can**
Nina ...... German.
items.explanation: Ability → Nina **can speak and understand** German.

items.sentence:
It wasn't necessary for us to print the tickets. **need**
We ...... to print the tickets.

It's possible that the message is from our teacher. **might**
The message ...... from our teacher.
items.explanation: Possibility → message **might be** from our teacher.

items.sentenceAfter: use your phone during the test. It's forbidden.

explanationContent.whyCorrect: The context points to "mustn't" — forbidden during test.

wrongOptions.B: "don't have to" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "mustn't" is correct.
wrongOptions.don't have to: "don't have to" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "mustn't" is correct.
wrongOptions.C: "shouldn't to" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "mustn't" is correct.
wrongOptions.shouldn't to: "shouldn't to" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "mustn't" is correct.
wrongOptions.D: "needn't" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "mustn't" is correct.
wrongOptions.needn't: "needn't" does not match the grammar or meaning clue in this sentence — "mustn't" is correct.

explanationContent.usefulTip: Eliminate options that break grammar first, then check which remaining word fits the meaning.
explanationContent.grammarFocus: Possessive forms and possessive determiners show who something belongs to.

exercises.title: E: Complete the sentences
exercises.instructions: Write one word in each gap.
exercises.studentInstruction: Write one word in each gap.

items.sentence: I need to ...... a message to the club secretary.
She ...... the envelope carefully before posting the letter.
Could you ...... this parcel to the front desk?
I ...... him a voice message, but he hasn't listened to it yet.
items.explanation: Past of send → **sent** him a voice message.

items.sentence: The package was ...... two days late.
He ...... a quick text to say he had arrived safely.
items.explanation: Past of send → **sent** a quick text.

items.sentence: She printed ...... the form and signed it.
items.explanation: Make a copy → printed **out** the form.

items.sentence: I've been trying to ...... in touch with my old classmate.
items.explanation: Stay in contact → **get** in touch with.

items.sentence: You must ...... in this application form before Friday.
items.explanation: Complete a form → **fill** in this application.

items.sentence: The reception desk is ...... a package for you.
He wrote the address on the ...... and posted the letter.
She forwarded the announcement to all her ...... .
items.explanation: Work colleagues → forwarded to all her **colleagues**.
